companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVIDIA's 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders is online tomorrow, June 24 at 9 a.m. PT — the first since Vera Rubin entered full production and the South Korea AI factory agreements were signed. ISC High Performance 2026 (Hamburg) featured NVIDIA announcing a record 35 AI HPC supercomputers in development across Europe, plus the Vera Rubin platform for scientific computing. NVIDIA XR AI entered public beta, giving developers a framework for multimodal AI agents for AR glasses and XR devices. The IREN data-center partnership (up to $2.1 billion investment option, announced June 22) extends NVIDIA's equity-anchored infrastructure strategy alongside its existing CoreWeave (~$2 billion) stake. Vera Rubin remains in production at Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceXAI.

items.headline: NVIDIA 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders is tomorrow — June 24 at 9 a.m. PT
items.detail: NVIDIA will hold its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders online tomorrow, June 24 at 9 a.m. PT. Non-stockholders may participate via guest login. The meeting comes as NVIDIA's FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue remains on track for approximately $56 billion, Vera Rubin systems are in full production at major AI labs, and the Korea AI factory agreements (SK Telecom, LG, SK hynix, NAVER, Hyundai) from Jensen Huang's June 5–8 Seoul visit are the freshest strategic developments.

items.headline: ISC High Performance 2026: record 35 NVIDIA AI HPC supercomputers in development across Europe
items.detail: At ISC High Performance 2026 in Hamburg, NVIDIA announced a record 35 NVIDIA AI HPC supercomputers are in development across Europe, equipping research institutions and national labs with next-generation AI infrastructure for continental scientific AI. NVIDIA also presented the Vera Rubin platform as the foundation for world-class supercomputers for science, reinforcing its parallel strategy of pairing commercial AI factory expansion with sovereign and academic deployments.

items.headline: NVIDIA XR AI enters public beta — multimodal AI agent framework for AR glasses and XR devices
items.detail: NVIDIA XR AI is now available in public beta, giving developers a framework for building multimodal AI agents for AR glasses and extended reality (XR) devices. The release expands NVIDIA's AI developer toolkit beyond data center and PC use cases into the growing wearable and spatial computing market, complementing the Windows-native RTX Spark agent work announced with Microsoft at Build 2026.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro is now inside its general availability window with seven days remaining on CEO Sundar Pichai's self-imposed June 30 deadline set at Google I/O. Pro targets a 2-million-token context window (double Flash), a Deep Think reasoning mode gated to the $250/month Ultra subscription tier, and frontier multimodal understanding at an expected ~$15/$60 per million input/output tokens. The model remains in limited Vertex AI enterprise preview only — not yet in the consumer Gemini app, Google AI Studio, or general API access. Apple's reported $1 billion per year Gemini deal for a rebuilt Siri AI is the largest known enterprise model distribution contract, targeting a September 2026 launch on Google Cloud NVIDIA Blackwell. Nobel laureate John Jumper's departure to Anthropic is a notable talent loss.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro enters GA window — 7 days to June 30 self-imposed deadline; limited Vertex AI enterprise preview only
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro is inside its general availability window as of today, June 23, with CEO Sundar Pichai's self-imposed June 30 deadline seven days away. The model remains in limited preview for select Vertex AI enterprise customers only — not yet available in the consumer Gemini app, Google AI Studio, or general API access. Pro targets a 2-million-token context window (double Flash's 1M), a Deep Think reasoning mode for the $250/month Ultra tier, and frontier multimodal understanding. Expected pricing is approximately $15 per million input tokens and $60 per million output tokens.

items.headline: Apple's reported $1B/year Gemini deal powers rebuilt Siri AI launching September 2026 on Google Cloud NVIDIA Blackwell
items.detail: Apple's reported $1 billion per year agreement for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI — announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8) — remains Google DeepMind's largest known enterprise model distribution contract. Siri AI is expected to launch September 2026 on Google Cloud infrastructure powered by NVIDIA Blackwell chips, reaching an estimated 2.2 billion active Apple devices. The EU launch is delayed pending unresolved Digital Markets Act regulatory talks.

items.headline: Android 17 launched June 16 with Gemini Omni; John Jumper's departure to Anthropic is notable research talent loss
items.detail: Google released Android 17 on June 16 to Pixel 6 through Pixel 10a devices, with Gemini Omni as the core AI upgrade enabling persistent context-aware AI assistance across the OS; third-party AI APIs are now open to developers. Separately, Nobel Prize–winning chemist John Jumper — co-creator of AlphaFold and 2024 Nobel laureate in chemistry — left Google DeepMind to join Anthropic (announced June 19–20), representing one of Google DeepMind's most high-profile recent research departures.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: July 1 is eight days away — the deadline for Copilot Cowork frontline tenants to complete transition to usage-based pricing (billing active since June 19), and the date Microsoft 365 Business Standard with Copilot and Business Premium with Copilot become permanent commercial SKUs with a global price update. Microsoft IQ (Work IQ + Web IQ) is GA across GitHub Copilot, Foundry, and Copilot Studio. Azure cloud services revenue grew 40% in recent quarters; capex rose 74% driven by AI infrastructure investment. Microsoft is exploring hosting a fine-tuned DeepSeek or comparable open-source model to reduce Copilot Cowork API costs, without affecting Claude's position in Foundry or GPT-5.5 as the primary Copilot foundation. New Agentic AI certifications AB-100 and AB-620 are entering the catalog as legacy Applied Skills retire June 30.

items.headline: July 1 deadline approaching — Copilot Cowork frontline transition; Microsoft 365 Business Standard/Premium with Copilot become permanent SKUs
items.detail: Frontline tenants have eight days — until July 1 — to complete transition to usage-based Copilot Cowork billing, which has been active since June 19. Microsoft 365 Business Standard with Copilot and Business Premium with Copilot formalize as permanent commercial SKUs on July 1 with a global price update. Legacy Copilot Applied Skills certifications retire June 30; new Agentic AI certifications AB-100 (Agentic AI Business Solutions Architect) and AB-620 (AI Agent Builder Associate) are entering the catalog.

items.headline: Microsoft IQ GA across GitHub Copilot, Foundry, and Copilot Studio; Azure grew 40% with capex up 74%
items.detail: Microsoft IQ — combining Work IQ APIs (organizational context inside the M365 trust boundary) and Web IQ (real-time web grounding) — is generally available across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio as of June 16. Azure cloud services revenue growth reached 40% in recent quarters, with Microsoft capex rising 74% driven by AI infrastructure investment. CEO Satya Nadella cited the AI portfolio's impact as the driver for continued capital deployment.

items.headline: Microsoft exploring fine-tuned DeepSeek for some Copilot Cowork workloads to reduce API costs
items.detail: Axios reported Microsoft is evaluating hosting a fine-tuned version of DeepSeek or a comparable open-source model as a lower-cost foundation for some Copilot Cowork workloads — reducing dependency on Anthropic and OpenAI API costs as agentic token consumption scales into enterprise deployments. The exploration does not affect Claude's position in Microsoft Foundry or GPT-5.5's status as the primary Copilot foundation model.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 has not shipped as of today (June 23) despite the prediction market window being open; prediction markets assign elevated odds for a June 22–28 release with no official OpenAI announcement. Patch the Planet and GPT-5.5-Cyber launched June 22 — a defensive cybersecurity initiative with Trail of Bits and HackerOne covering 30+ open-source projects under the Daybreak platform. The 42-state attorney general probe (June 12) and CFO Sarah Friar's reported concern about 2026 IPO readiness are the key pre-IPO headwinds. Noam Shazeer — co-inventor of the Transformer attention mechanism — joined OpenAI June 18 as Lead for Architecture Research. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% in late May — the first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%. OpenAI's confidential S-1 was filed June 8.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 launch window open — no official announcement as of June 23; prediction markets elevated for June 22–28 release
items.detail: As of June 23, OpenAI has made no official announcement about GPT-5.6. Prediction markets assign elevated odds to a release in the June 22–28 window. GPT-5.6 — described by chief scientist Jakub Pachocki as a 'meaningful improvement' — is expected to feature a redesigned reward audit pipeline addressing alignment issues, a 1.5-million-token context window (43% larger than GPT-5.5), and 10–15% token efficiency gains. Noam Shazeer — co-inventor of the Transformer attention mechanism and co-author of 'Attention Is All You Need' — joined OpenAI June 18 as Lead for Architecture Research, its highest-profile researcher hire in years.

items.headline: 42-state AG probe, potential CFO IPO delay, and ChatGPT market share drop are key pre-IPO headwinds
items.detail: A 42-state attorney general coalition led by NY AG Letitia James opened a formal probe into OpenAI on June 12, four days after its S-1 filing; subpoenas have been served. CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly expressed concern about operational and compliance readiness, raising the possibility of a delay to 2027. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% in late May — the first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%. EU AI Act chatbot enforcement begins August 2, 2026, adding compliance milestones to the pre-IPO period.

items.headline: Patch the Planet and GPT-5.5-Cyber launched June 22 for open-source defensive security
items.detail: OpenAI launched 'Patch the Planet' on June 22 — a cybersecurity initiative with Trail of Bits and HackerOne to find and fix vulnerabilities in widely used open-source software. More than 30 projects are participating, including cURL, Go, Python, and Sigstore. OpenAI also released GPT-5.5-Cyber, a specialized defensive security model, and a Codex Security plugin for vulnerability scanning inside developer workflows — all under the Daybreak cybersecurity platform.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Fable 5 credit-based billing begins today, June 23 — $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens — though Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain suspended globally (day 11 of the export control directive). Subscribers received approximately 4–5 days of actual Fable 5 access rather than the advertised 13-day trial window, with Anthropic issuing no guidance on the billing gap. Trump's post-G7 Axios statement (June 20) that Anthropic is no longer a national security threat remains the clearest political signal; no formal Commerce Department withdrawal has been issued. Fable 5 resurfaced in the Claude Android app's coding interface on June 21 with a 'rate-limiting' error — the strongest early-restoration signal yet. John Jumper (AlphaFold co-creator and 2024 Nobel laureate) joined Anthropic; a science event is scheduled June 30. Revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion. Anthropic's July 8 biometric ID verification policy creates a structured path for US citizen access without requiring a full Commerce Department reversal.

items.headline: Fable 5 credit billing starts today (June 23) at $10/$50 per million tokens; day 11 of suspension; subscribers received ~4–5 days of access instead of 13
items.detail: Starting today, June 23, Claude Fable 5 is billed at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens — exactly double the cost of Claude Opus 4.8. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain suspended globally under the US government export control directive (day 11). Subscribers received approximately 4–5 days of actual Fable 5 access rather than the advertised 13-day trial, as the models were offline from June 12 to approximately June 18. Anthropic has issued no updated pricing guidance on how the billing gap will be handled.

items.headline: Fable 5 resurfaced in Android app June 21 with 'rate-limiting' error; July 8 biometric ID policy offers structured US citizen restoration path
items.detail: On June 21, developers reported Fable 5 reappearing in the Claude Android app's coding interface model selector — invocation attempts returned 'server is temporarily rate-limiting requests' rather than the prior hard-offline message, the strongest restoration signal yet. Trump told Axios (June 20) he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat, but no formal Commerce Department withdrawal has been issued. Anthropic's updated privacy policy effective July 8 expands collection of government-issued ID and biometric facial geometry via Persona — creating a compliant restoration path for verified US citizens. Polymarket assigns 57% odds of US restoration before July 1.

items.headline: John Jumper (AlphaFold co-creator, 2024 Nobel laureate) joined Anthropic; science event June 30; revenue run rate surpassed $30B
items.detail: John Jumper, co-creator of AlphaFold and 2024 Nobel Prize laureate in chemistry, left Google DeepMind to join Anthropic — one of Anthropic's most prominent research hires, signaling expansion into scientific AI. Anthropic is hosting a science-focused event on June 30, likely related to the hire. Anthropic's revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion. The confidential draft Form S-1 was submitted to the SEC on June 1, 2026, after closing a $65 billion Series H at a ~$965 billion post-money valuation.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta Llama 4 continues to be actively evaluated as the leading enterprise self-hostable alternative during the Fable 5/Mythos 5 suspension (day 11). Meta is testing a 'Meta One' subscription umbrella unifying Facebook Plus, Instagram Plus, WhatsApp Plus, and AI product subscriptions. Meta AI Mode on Facebook (launched June 15) synthesizes answers from public posts across Groups and Reels for Meta's 3.2 billion daily active users. Meta's 2026 capex guidance of $115–135 billion is unchanged; Broadcom reconfirmed Meta as one of its six core custom-silicon customers.

items.headline: Meta Llama 4 positioned as primary enterprise self-hostable alternative as Fable 5 ban enters day 11
items.detail: As the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 global suspension enters day 11, Meta Llama 4 has emerged as the primary self-hostable frontier-class model drawing enterprise evaluation. Enterprises seeking to reduce exposure to government model access revocation risk are evaluating Llama 4's open-weights posture as a hedge. Mistral AI's CEO Arthur Mensch has made similar sovereignty arguments; both reflect the industry's acute awareness of model access risk as a procurement variable following the June 12 suspension.

items.headline: Meta testing 'Meta One' subscription umbrella to unify Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and AI product subscriptions
items.detail: Meta is testing a 'Meta One' subscription brand designed to unify Facebook Plus, Instagram Plus, WhatsApp Plus, and forthcoming AI product subscriptions into a single membership tier. The move is part of Meta's push to build sustainable subscription revenue alongside advertising as 2026 capex of $115–135 billion scales.

items.headline: Meta AI Mode on Facebook synthesizes answers from public posts across Groups and Reels for 3.2B DAUs
items.detail: Meta AI Mode on Facebook — launched June 15 — lets users ask plain-language questions and receive synthesized answers drawn from public posts, Groups, and Reels across the platform. The feature uses Meta AI to surface community-sourced knowledge for Meta's 3.2 billion daily active users, positioning Facebook as an AI-powered information layer alongside its traditional social feed.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS Summit New York (June 17–20) concluded with four flagship enterprise releases: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore GA (Managed Knowledge Base, Web Search, Agent Optimization, A/B testing, intent analytics, Guardrails for prompt injection), AWS Continuum (AI-native security), AWS Context (knowledge graph for agents), and EC2 G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs (4.6x AI inference). Amazon Bedrock now serves as the enterprise multi-model control plane hosting Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT-5.5, and xAI Grok 4.3 under unified AWS governance. Southwest Airlines named AWS as its preferred cloud partner for AI-enabled architecture by 2028. Cerebras Systems Q1 2026 earnings release after market close today (June 23).

items.headline: AWS Summit NYC concluded June 20 with AgentCore GA, AWS Continuum, AWS Context, and EC2 G7 as flagship enterprise releases
items.detail: AWS Summit New York (June 17–20) produced four flagship enterprise releases: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore GA (with Managed Knowledge Base, Web Search, Agent Optimization, A/B testing, intent analytics, and Guardrails for prompt injection), AWS Continuum (AI-native security for agentic workloads), AWS Context (knowledge graph for agents), and EC2 G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs delivering 4.6x AI inference performance — making AWS the first major cloud to support this GPU class.

items.headline: Amazon Bedrock is the enterprise multi-model control plane hosting Claude, GPT-5.5, and Grok 4.3 under unified governance
items.detail: Following AgentCore GA, Grok 4.3 on Bedrock (June 15), and OpenAI frontier models and Codex (June 1), Amazon Bedrock serves as the enterprise control plane where companies access Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT-5.5, and xAI Grok under unified AWS governance, compliance, billing, and security. AgentCore provides the runtime for multi-model agentic workflows, positioning Bedrock as the enterprise multi-model distribution hub.

items.headline: Southwest Airlines named AWS preferred cloud partner for AI-enabled architecture by 2028
items.detail: Southwest Airlines announced AWS as its preferred cloud provider to modernize its technology foundation and apply AI, with plans to transition to a cloud-based, AI- and agent-enabled architecture on AWS by 2028. The airline partnership reflects AWS's continued expansion beyond hyperscale tech into regulated, logistics-intensive enterprise verticals.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: SpaceX signed a $6.3 billion compute deal with open-source AI startup Reflection AI — Colossus 2's first major third-party customer, paying $150 million per month starting July 1 through 2029 for NVIDIA GB300 chips near Memphis. This deal reveals the pricing scale of prior Colossus agreements: Anthropic at approximately $1.25 billion per month and Google at approximately $920 million per month. SPCX stock fell for a third consecutive day amid a debut bond issuance that sparked market concern; the stock has corrected significantly from its June 16 peak of $225.64. The $60 billion all-stock Anysphere (Cursor AI) acquisition is expected to close Q3 2026. Grok for Microsoft Word (free add-in) remains live; Grok 4.3 is GA on Amazon Bedrock.

items.headline: SpaceX signed $6.3B Reflection AI compute deal — reveals Anthropic at $1.25B/month and Google at $920M/month for Colossus
items.detail: SpaceX signed a $6.3 billion compute deal with open-source AI startup Reflection AI for access to Colossus 2 — $150 million per month starting July 1 through 2029 for NVIDIA GB300 chips. Either party can terminate with 90 days' notice after the initial three months. The deal is smaller than SpaceX's prior Colossus agreements: Anthropic at approximately $1.25 billion per month and Google at approximately $920 million per month, according to CNBC. Reflection AI is using the compute deal to advance its open-weight AI strategy as an alternative to closed frontier labs.

items.headline: SPCX fell for third consecutive day; debut bond issuance sparked market concern
items.detail: SPCX stock fell for a third consecutive day as SpaceX issued its debut bond shortly after the June 12 IPO — a move that surprised some investors and sparked concern about leverage. The stock has corrected significantly from its June 16 all-time high of $225.64; IPO price was $135. The $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI), confirmed via SEC Form 8-K, is expected to receive regulatory clearance and close Q3 2026. Cursor had over 1 million paying users, more than half the Fortune 500 as clients, and more than $2 billion ARR.

items.headline: Grok 4.3 GA on Amazon Bedrock; Grok for Microsoft Word free add-in; Gopuff AI shopping assistant live
items.detail: Grok 4.3 is generally available on Amazon Bedrock with a 1-million-token context window, the lowest reported hallucination rate among frontier models, and configurable reasoning effort. Grok for Microsoft Word — a free Microsoft 365 add-in launched June 18 — embeds live web research, Mermaid-based diagram generation, and conversational AI directly into Word's side panel (PowerPoint and Excel versions also available). Grok also powers Gopuff's 'Go' AI shopping assistant and is natively available on Databricks Agent Bricks.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: Broadcom (AVGO) passed its ex-dividend date yesterday (June 22), with a $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend payable June 30 — marking 14 consecutive years of dividend increases. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue hit $10.8 billion (+143% YoY) on record quarterly revenue, operating profit, and free cash flow; Q3 guidance is approximately $16 billion; FY2026 target is approximately $56 billion; FY2027 target exceeds $100 billion across six core custom-silicon customers including Anthropic, Google, and Meta. Broadcom's Tomahawk 6 (Davisson) — the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics — is the most significant new product.

items.headline: Broadcom Q2 FY2026 AI revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY); Q3 guidance ~$16B; FY2026 target ~$56B; FY2027 >$100B; $0.65/share dividend payable June 30
items.detail: Broadcom (AVGO) ex-dividend date passed June 22, with a $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend payable June 30. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue hit $10.8 billion on record quarterly revenue, operating profit, and free cash flow. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance is approximately $16 billion. Broadcom's FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue target remains approximately $56 billion, and the FY2027 target exceeds $100 billion driven by six core custom-silicon customers. The dividend marks 14 consecutive years of increases.

items.headline: Broadcom Tomahawk 6 (Davisson): industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics
items.detail: The Tomahawk 6 (codename Davisson), unveiled in mid-June 2026, is the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch silicon with co-packaged optics, addressing bandwidth demands of AI data centers where conventional pluggable optics are insufficient at hyperscale. JPMorgan maintains an 'aggressive buy' rating on AVGO; 48 analysts hold a 'Strong Buy' consensus.

items.headline: Broadcom has six core custom-silicon customers including Anthropic, Google, and Meta; FY2027 target exceeds $100B
items.detail: Broadcom reconfirmed six core custom-silicon customers in its Q2 FY2026 results. Anthropic, Google, and Meta are publicly identified as major partners — with Anthropic securing multiple gigawatts of TPU capacity via the Google-Broadcom agreement, and Meta committing to 1 gigawatt of custom MTIA chips. Broadcom's FY2027 AI semiconductor revenue target exceeds $100 billion, driven by multi-year multi-generation silicon partnerships with all six customers.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: Palantir Q1 2026 revenue jumped 85% year-over-year to $1.6 billion while US commercial growth hit 133%, driving a 10-point full-year guidance raise. Maven Smart System usage quadrupled over 12 months and retains DoD program-of-record status. AIPCon 10 (June 4) showcased AI amplifying tradecraft at Kirkland & Ellis, McCarthy Building, USDA, Hertz, and others. Palantir AIP is now available on Google Cloud Marketplace with BigQuery and Gemini integrations. The Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline remains under strain from the Fable/Mythos suspension (day 11); Trump's June 20 statement has not yet produced a formal Commerce Department reversal.

items.headline: Palantir Q1 revenue +85% YoY to $1.6B; US commercial +133%; Maven usage quadrupled over 12 months
items.detail: Palantir reported Q1 2026 revenue of $1.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year, with US commercial revenue growing 133% — the fastest commercial growth in company history. Maven Smart System usage quadrupled over the prior 12 months. Palantir raised its full-year guidance by 10 points following the results, reflecting AIP's accelerating enterprise and government deployment momentum.

items.headline: Maven Smart System retains DoD program-of-record status; Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline under strain
items.detail: Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation with multi-year protected funding across five military branches. The Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline remains under strain from the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export control suspension (day 11). Trump's June 20 Axios statement that Anthropic is no longer a national security threat has not yet produced a formal Commerce Department reversal or restoration of Pentagon-Anthropic supply chain access. Claude remains integrated in Maven for eligible workflows pending resolution.

items.headline: Palantir AIPCon 10 showcased AI amplifying customer tradecraft; AIP available on Google Cloud Marketplace
items.detail: Palantir hosted AIPCon 10 on June 4, 2026, featuring customer-led demonstrations from Kirkland & Ellis, McCarthy Building, USDA, Hertz, Nscale, Accenture, and Parts Town. Palantir AIP is now available on Google Cloud Marketplace with BigQuery data access and Gemini model integrations, expanding cloud distribution beyond AWS and Azure.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: Apple is reportedly in early-stage internal discussions about acquiring Perplexity AI at approximately $14 billion — which would be Apple's largest acquisition ever. Apple also considered acquiring Mistral AI. The $1 billion per year Google Gemini deal powering a rebuilt Siri AI remains the defining Apple AI story with a September 2026 launch on Google Cloud NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (allowing ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remains disabled. Bloomberg reports OpenAI is weighing a breach-of-contract notice over the perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement.

items.headline: Apple in early internal discussions about acquiring Perplexity AI at ~$14B; also considered Mistral AI
items.detail: Bloomberg reports Apple executives including Adrian Perica and Eddy Cue held early internal discussions about potentially acquiring Perplexity AI at approximately $14 billion — which would be Apple's largest acquisition ever, dwarfing the $3 billion Beats deal in 2014. Discussions are described as early-stage and 'may not lead to an offer.' Perplexity has stated it is unaware of ongoing or prospective M&A talks. Sherwood News also reported Apple considered acquiring Mistral AI, suggesting Apple is actively evaluating multiple AI acquisition paths as it diversifies beyond the Gemini-for-Siri partnership.

items.headline: Apple's $1B/year Gemini deal powers rebuilt Siri AI launching September 2026; iOS 27 Extensions still disabled
items.detail: Apple's reported $1 billion per year deal for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI — announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8) — remains active with a September 2026 launch target on Google Cloud NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure. The iOS 27 developer beta contains a working Extensions framework that would allow users to choose ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri, but Apple has disabled it pending DMA regulatory clarity and resolution with OpenAI, which Bloomberg reports is weighing a breach-of-contract notice.

items.headline: OpenAI reportedly weighing breach-of-contract notice over lack of prime ChatGPT placement in iOS 27 Siri AI
items.detail: Bloomberg reports OpenAI is weighing a breach-of-contract notice related to Apple's WWDC 2026 announcement that Google Gemini — rather than ChatGPT — would power a rebuilt Siri AI. Apple's existing OpenAI integration bringing ChatGPT into Siri and Apple Intelligence remains in place, but the Gemini deal effectively displaced OpenAI as the primary Siri AI engine. Apple CFO Kevan Parekh mentioned Perplexity's Personal Computer product at Q2 2026 earnings, signaling further AI platform diversification.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 GPU samples are shipping to lead customers including OpenAI and Meta. AMD denied MI455X delay reports, stating Helios rack systems remain on target for H2 2026. The MEXT acquisition (completed June 15) brings predictive memory technology making flash storage behave like DRAM for AI workloads. Rackspace signed a 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment agreement June 16. AMD Advancing AI 2026 is a two-day event on July 22–23 in San Francisco — the next major disclosure milestone for MI450, Helios confirmation, MI500 preview, and OpenAI/Meta deployment details. AMD reports Q2 2026 results August 4.

items.headline: AMD MI450 GPU samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta; Helios on target for H2 2026 despite delay reports
items.detail: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 GPUs are sampling to lead customers including OpenAI and Meta. AMD denied reports of MI455X delays, stating Helios rack systems — AMD's NVIDIA Vera Rubin competitor — remain on target for H2 2026 production. MI450 delivers up to 40 PFLOPs FP4 performance. Early customer engagement on the successor MI500 architecture is also active.

items.headline: AMD completed MEXT acquisition June 15; signed Rackspace 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment June 16
items.detail: AMD completed the acquisition of MEXT on June 15, bringing predictive memory technology that makes flash storage behave like DRAM for AI workloads — a critical bottleneck for large-scale inference. On June 16, AMD signed a definitive agreement with Rackspace Technology for a phased 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment, adding cloud capacity for MI450-class workloads.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22–23 at Moscone Center, San Francisco; Q2 results August 4
items.detail: AMD's Advancing AI 2026 event is a two-day in-person and livestreamed event on July 22–23 at Moscone Center in San Francisco — the next major disclosure milestone. Expected: formal MI450 launch, EPYC Venice Zen 6 CPUs, Helios rack confirmation, and MI500 series preview. AMD also reports Q2 2026 financial results on August 4. The AMD-OpenAI (6GW, MI450 beginning H2 2026) and AMD-Meta (6GW, ~$60B) strategic partnership milestones are most likely to feature.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Apple is reportedly in early internal discussions about a ~$14 billion acquisition of Perplexity AI — which would be Apple's largest deal ever. CEO Aravind Srinivas has not confirmed acquisition discussions and maintains the company's stated preference for a 2028 IPO. Perplexity's $500M ARR (April 2026, 335% YoY growth), 100M+ MAU, and $20B valuation from the May 2026 round represent the current price anchors. Perplexity Computer is live as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams; the Comet AI browser reached #3 in the App Store within 48 hours of its iOS launch.

items.headline: Apple reportedly considering ~$14B Perplexity AI acquisition in early-stage internal discussions; Perplexity denies awareness; CEO reaffirms 2028 IPO
items.detail: Bloomberg reports Apple executives Adrian Perica and Eddy Cue held early internal discussions about potentially acquiring Perplexity AI at approximately $14 billion. Perplexity confirmed it is unaware of ongoing or prospective M&A talks. CEO Aravind Srinivas publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO on June 9. Apple's parallel consideration of Mistral AI suggests the Perplexity discussions are not exclusive.

items.headline: CEO Srinivas confirmed 2028 IPO target; $500M ARR (335% YoY), 100M+ MAU, $20B valuation from May 2026 round
items.detail: CEO Aravind Srinivas confirmed the 2028 IPO target on June 9, 2026 as a deliberate strategy regardless of Anthropic and OpenAI moving toward public markets. ARR reached $500 million in April 2026 (335% year-over-year growth), with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20B valuation from the May 2026 fundraising round.

items.headline: Perplexity Computer live as native Microsoft 365 side panels; Comet browser reached #3 App Store on iOS launch
items.detail: Perplexity's Computer agentic tool is live as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams — directly competing with Microsoft Copilot inside Microsoft 365. The Comet AI browser is globally available and reached the #3 App Store position within 48 hours of its iOS launch, positioning Perplexity as a workplace AI layer and browser-level intelligence platform.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CoreWeave (CRWV) joined the Nasdaq-100 yesterday, June 22 — closing +9.67% at $117.03 on mandatory QQQ and Nasdaq-100 tracking fund buying. Russell 3000 inclusion follows June 27. Contracted revenue backlog is approaching approximately $131 billion (up ~$32B since end-Q1). The 2026 capex floor was raised to $31 billion for Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout. A $2.2 billion Chicago-area data-center lease adds contracted revenue visibility. Cerebras Systems Q1 2026 earnings — the next major AI infrastructure public-company earnings catalyst — release after market close tonight (June 23).

items.headline: CRWV closed +9.67% at $117.03 on Nasdaq-100 inclusion day (June 22); Russell 3000 follows June 27
items.detail: CoreWeave (CRWV) officially joined the Nasdaq-100 Index before market open yesterday, June 22, 2026 — just 15 months after its IPO at $40 per share, among the fastest index inclusions in recent tech sector history. CRWV closed at $117.03, up 9.67%, as mandatory buying from QQQ and Nasdaq-100 tracking funds (representing $662B+ in tracked assets) executed. Other June 22 additions: Astera Labs, Nebius Group, Rocket Lab, and Teradyne. Russell 3000 inclusion follows June 27.

items.headline: Cerebras Systems Q1 2026 earnings releasing tonight (June 23) — first public earnings; analysts estimate $56.65M revenue, loss of $0.14/share
items.detail: Cerebras Systems (CBRS) releases its Q1 2026 financial results after market close tonight, June 23 — the company's first quarterly report since its Nasdaq IPO in May 2026. Analysts estimate revenue of approximately $56.65M and a loss of $0.14/share. Key investor focus: how fast the $24.6 billion backlog is converting to revenue, whether the company has found major customers outside Abu Dhabi, and progress on the AWS Trainium/CS-3 inference collaboration announced in March 2026. The earnings call is at 2 pm PT / 5 pm ET.

items.headline: CoreWeave backlog approaching $131B; capex floor raised to $31B; $2.2B Chicago data-center lease
items.detail: Analyst estimates put CoreWeave's contracted revenue backlog at approximately $131 billion, up roughly $32 billion since reporting $99.4 billion at end-Q1 2026. The 2026 capex floor was raised to $31 billion to accommodate Vera Rubin NVL72 commitments from Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. A new $2.2 billion Chicago-area data-center lease adds contracted revenue visibility and directly addresses the bear case on CoreWeave's spending profile.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral AI confirmed a $3.5 billion raise at a €20 billion valuation. Its Paris-area data center at Bruyères-le-Châtel is expected to open by end of June 2026 with 13,800–18,000 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems — now fewer than 8 days away. The NVIDIA-Mistral Mistral Compute sovereign AI GPU cloud for European customers launched at VivaTech June 20. Mistral launched Vibe — a unified AI agent with Work Mode and Code Mode, VS Code extension, and CLI. Mistral acquired Emmi AI (Austrian industrial AI simulation). CEO Arthur Mensch continues to leverage European concern over the Fable ban to position Mistral as the open-source sovereignty alternative. Mistral targets €1 billion in 2026 revenue with 1,000 employees.

items.headline: Mistral confirmed $3.5B raise at €20B valuation; Paris data center opening by end of June with 13,800+ NVIDIA GB300 systems
items.detail: Mistral AI confirmed a $3.5 billion fundraise at a €20 billion valuation — nearly doubling its prior €11.7 billion September 2025 Series C. The Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris is expected to open by end of June 2026 with 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems, forming the first node in a planned 200 MW European compute roadmap. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10 MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. CEO Arthur Mensch confirmed 1,000 employees and a €1 billion 2026 revenue target.

items.headline: Mistral Compute sovereign AI GPU cloud launched at VivaTech; Vibe agent and Emmi AI acquisition announced
items.detail: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (closing June 20). Jensen Huang highlighted Mistral Compute as Europe's AI sovereignty infrastructure example. Mistral also launched Vibe, a unified AI agent combining Work Mode (long-running tasks) and Code Mode (remote coding and pull requests) with a VS Code extension and CLI. Mistral acquired Austrian company Emmi AI, which develops AI simulation models for industrial engineering.

items.headline: CEO Mensch positions Mistral as open-source sovereignty alternative to US-controlled models amid Fable ban
items.detail: CEO Arthur Mensch is actively leveraging European concern over the Anthropic Fable/Mythos export suspension to position Mistral as the open-source alternative that governments cannot revoke. Mensch stated: 'We exist to make sure that everyone gets access to the best AI systems, outside of centralised control exercised by states or corporations.' Enterprise teams are actively evaluating Llama 4 and Mistral as sovereign alternatives during the ban.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: Cybercab production at Giga Texas continues to ramp with 102+ units in the outbound lot; EPA filing (June 19) confirmed full vehicle specs (47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range, 3,113 lb curb weight). Both a Cybercab and a Cybertruck have been spotted with robotaxi validation hardware, suggesting Tesla is broadening autonomous ride-hailing validation beyond the Cybercab alone. Cybercabs are expected to enter active fleet service as early as July 2026. The robotaxi service covers Austin (full metro), Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. Optimus Gen 3 is in mass production at Fremont; the dedicated Optimus factory at Giga Texas North Campus targets 10 million robots per year at $20K–$30K for public sales by end of 2027. FSD v14.3 reduces inference latency 20%.

items.headline: Cybercab production ramp: 102+ units in Giga Texas outbound lot; EPA confirms specs; fleet service entering July 2026
items.detail: As of June 19, 2026, 102 Cybercab units were in the outbound lot at Giga Texas. The EPA filing confirmed full vehicle specifications: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range (293-mile adjusted), and 3,113 lb curb weight. Both a Cybercab and a Cybertruck have been spotted with specialized robotaxi validation hardware on their roofs, indicating Tesla is broadening autonomous ride-hailing validation to multiple vehicle platforms. Cybercabs are expected to enter active fleet service as early as July 2026.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 in mass production at Fremont; dedicated Optimus factory at Giga Texas targeting 10M robots/year
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot entered mass production at Fremont in January 2026, with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026. Tesla's Q1 2026 report confirmed a 5.2 million square foot Optimus factory expansion at Gigafactory Texas North Campus targeting 10 million robots per year. Public Optimus sales target end of 2027 at $20,000–$30,000.

items.headline: FSD v14.3 cuts inference latency 20%; robotaxi service active in Austin, Dallas, Houston, and SF Bay Area
items.detail: FSD (Supervised) v14.3 launched in April 2026 with a rewritten AI compiler reducing inference latency by up to 20% and an enhanced neural network vision encoder for low-visibility scenarios. The Cybercab robotaxi service covers the full Austin metro area, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. Early users and the insurance industry have reported some long wait times and stalled rides, which Tesla has not formally addressed.

ipoTracker.status: Confirmed filing; Fable 5 credit model starts today (June 23) at $10/$50 per million tokens; models still offline (day 11); July 8 biometric ID policy as structured restoration path; 57% odds of US restoration by July 1
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, after closing a $65 billion Series H at a ~$965 billion post-money valuation. An October 2026 listing window is widely reported. Fable 5 credit-based billing begins today (June 23) at $10/$50 per million input/output tokens, though Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain suspended globally (day 11). Subscribers received approximately 4–5 days of actual Fable 5 access rather than the advertised 13-day trial. Trump told Axios (June 20) he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat, but no formal Commerce Department reversal has been issued. Anthropic's July 8 biometric ID policy creates a structured restoration path for verified US citizens. Polymarket gives 57% odds of US restoration before July 1. Revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion.
ipoTracker.note: Fable 5 credit billing starts today (June 23). July 8 biometric ID policy is the next structured restoration pathway milestone. Science event June 30 may include notable announcements given John Jumper's hire.

latestArticle.title: AI News Today June 23 2026: Fable 5 Moves to Paid Access

ipoTracker.status: Confirmed SEC filing June 8; GPT-5.6 window open with no official announcement; 42-state AG probe filed June 12; Noam Shazeer joined June 18; CFO may push to 2027; Patch the Planet launched June 22
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI confirmed its confidential S-1 filing with the SEC on June 8, 2026. Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan are leading. CEO Sam Altman expects a listing within the next year; CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly expressed concern about operational readiness, with a potential delay to 2027. A coalition of 42 state attorneys general (led by NY AG Letitia James) opened a formal probe into OpenAI on June 12 — the most significant pre-IPO legal headwind. GPT-5.6's prediction market window remains open with elevated odds for a June 22–28 release; no official announcement as of June 23. Noam Shazeer joined June 18 as Lead for Architecture Research. EU AI Act chatbot enforcement begins August 2, 2026.
ipoTracker.note: GPT-5.6 window still open — watch for announcement. The 42-state AG probe and potential CFO push to 2027 are the key pre-IPO risk factors. Patch the Planet (June 22) is the most recent positive product development.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI's CFO reportedly wants to delay the IPO from 2026 to 2027

ipoTracker.status: SPCX fell third consecutive day; debut bond sparked concern; $6.3B Reflection AI compute deal signed; $60B Cursor acquisition pending Q3 close
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16 before a three-day losing streak driven in part by a debut bond issuance that sparked market concern. SpaceX confirmed via SEC Form 8-K the $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI), expected to close Q3 2026. SpaceX signed a $6.3 billion compute deal with Reflection AI for Colossus 2 access at $150M/month starting July 1 through 2029. xAI was folded into SpaceX as the SpaceXAI division in February 2026; all Grok/AI exposure trades via ticker SPCX.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: Reflection AI deal (June 22) indirectly confirmed Colossus pricing: Anthropic at $1.25B/month, Google at $920M/month. Cursor acquisition (Q3 2026 expected close) and Grok for Word (free) are the key near-term product milestones. SPCX stock correction from peak warrants monitoring.

Analysts estimate revenue of approximately $56.65M and a loss of $0.14/share. Key investor focus: how fast the $24.6 billion backlog is converting to revenue, and whether the company has found a major customer outside Abu Dhabi. Earnings call at 2 pm PT / 5 pm ET tonight.
ipoTracker.note: Q1 2026 earnings release is tonight (June 23) — the primary AI infrastructure earnings catalyst of the day. Results will set the tone for near-term CBRS trading and contextualize CoreWeave's growth trajectory.

The company has a $5.4 billion annualized revenue run-rate growing 65% YoY with positive free cash flow and a $134 billion valuation from its December 2025 Series L. The 2026 Data + AI Summit (June 18) featured SpaceXAI announcing native Grok availability on Databricks Agent Bricks.

ipoTracker.status: No confirmed filing; Apple reportedly in early acquisition discussions at ~$14B; Perplexity denies awareness of M&A talks; CEO reaffirmed 2028 IPO target June 9
ipoTracker.process: Apple executives including Adrian Perica and Eddy Cue reportedly held early internal discussions about acquiring Perplexity AI at approximately $14 billion. Perplexity confirmed it is unaware of ongoing or prospective M&A talks; CEO Aravind Srinivas publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO. ARR reached $500 million (April 2026, 335% YoY growth), with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20B valuation from the May 2026 round. Apple's parallel consideration of Mistral AI suggests the Perplexity talks are not exclusive.
ipoTracker.confidence: No filing; Apple acquisition reported as early-stage internal discussions; Perplexity denies awareness
ipoTracker.note: Perplexity's formal denial of M&A awareness and CEO's stated 2028 IPO preference remain the company's public position. Apple's dual consideration of Perplexity and Mistral reflects AI platform diversification strategy rather than an imminent deal.

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed compute partnership; Reflection AI deal (June 22) revealed Anthropic at ~$1.25B/month for Colossus; Fable/Mythos suspension does not affect compute terms
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceXAI (AI division of publicly traded SpaceX/SPCX) signed an agreement to provide Anthropic access to Colossus 1. SpaceX's June 22 Reflection AI deal ($6.3B, $150M/month) indirectly confirmed the pricing scale of Anthropic's Colossus agreement at approximately $1.25 billion per month — and Google's at approximately $920 million per month — per CNBC reporting. Anthropic expressed interest in developing multiple gigawatts of orbital AI compute capacity. Public financial terms for the Anthropic-SpaceXAI agreement were not disclosed in the original official announcement.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed relationship; Colossus pricing level confirmed indirectly via CNBC Reflection AI reporting
partnershipTracker.note: The Reflection AI deal (June 22) is the most significant new context for this partnership — it reveals Anthropic's approximate Colossus compute cost at $1.25B/month. Fable 5 credit billing starts today (June 23); no impact on compute contract.

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed expanded collaboration; AgentCore GA deepens enterprise deployment layer; Fable ban does not affect other Claude models on Bedrock
partnershipTracker.terms: The April 2026 expanded agreement commits more than $100 billion over 10 years, securing up to 5GW of Trainium2, Trainium3, and Trainium4 capacity. Project Rainier already runs more than one million Trainium2 chips for Claude workloads. AgentCore GA at AWS Summit NYC (June 17–20) incorporates Managed Knowledge Base, Web Search, Agent Optimization, and Guardrails as the enterprise deployment layer for Claude on Bedrock. Amazon confirmed Claude models (outside Fable 5 and Mythos 5) remain available to AWS customers.
partnershipTracker.note: AgentCore GA at AWS Summit NYC is the most significant recent operational development. No new contract terms. The Fable/Mythos suspension does not affect other Claude models on Bedrock.

Broadcom reconfirmed Anthropic as one of its six core custom-silicon customers; FY2026 AI target is $56 billion and FY2027 exceeds $100 billion. Claude Sonnet 4.5, Haiku 4.5, and Opus 4.1 are in public preview in Microsoft Foundry. NVIDIA's Vera CPUs are in production at Anthropic. Microsoft IQ is now GA across Foundry, enabling richer enterprise agent deployments. The Fable/Mythos suspension does not affect other Claude models in Foundry.
partnershipTracker.note: Microsoft IQ GA (June 16) is the most recent operational development. Microsoft's exploration of open-source model alternatives for Copilot Cowork does not affect Claude's position in Foundry.

Microsoft is exploring open-source model alternatives for some Copilot Cowork workloads — a potential future tension point.

Apple's reported $1B/year Google Gemini deal (announced WWDC June 8) effectively displaced OpenAI as the primary Siri AI engine. The iOS 27 developer beta contains a working Extensions framework letting users choose ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri, but Apple has disabled it. Bloomberg reports OpenAI is weighing a breach-of-contract notice over perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement.

latestArticle.title: AMD and Meta Announce Expanded Strategic Partnership to Deploy 6 Gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.type: Infrastructure equity investment and multi-generational GPU supply
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed deep strategic partnership — Nasdaq-100 inclusion completed June 22; Russell 3000 June 27; backlog approaching $131B; NVIDIA Annual Meeting tomorrow
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA invested roughly $2 billion in CoreWeave Class A common stock (~11% stake) and the companies committed to accelerating more than 5GW of AI factories by 2030. CoreWeave completed the industry-first Vera Rubin NVL72 bring-up on June 1. The AI compute backlog is approaching approximately $131 billion, driven by Vera Rubin NVL72 commitments from Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. CoreWeave joined the Nasdaq-100 on June 22.
partnershipTracker.note: Nasdaq-100 inclusion completed June 22. Russell 3000 follows June 27. NVIDIA Annual Meeting is tomorrow (June 24). NVIDIA's Korea AI factory expansion and IREN partnership add to the global GPU supply chain benefiting CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave to Join Nasdaq-100 Index

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed partnership — Mistral Compute launched at VivaTech June 20; Paris data center opening by end of June
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (closing June 20). Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris is expected to open by end of June 2026 with 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 systems, forming the first node in a planned 200 MW European compute roadmap. NVIDIA reaffirmed Mistral as the flagship of France's sovereign AI strategy.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies at VivaTech
partnershipTracker.note: Mistral Compute launched June 20. Paris data center opening is expected by end of June — within 7 days. Mistral's Vibe agent and Emmi AI acquisition (industrial AI simulation) are the most recent product developments.

latestArticle.title: France Advances Europe's AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies
